eras.name: The Spanish Golden Age
eras.description: Spain dominated European fashion with a somber, rigid, and aristocratic aesthetic. The use of black dye became a status symbol, reflecting the piety and power of the Habsburg court.
eras.aiImagePrompt: A high-ranking 16th-century Spanish nobleman wearing a stiff black velvet doublet with gold embroidery, an enormous white pleated ruff, and a short cape, set against a dark moody background in the style of Velázquez.

eras.name: The Majo and Maja Era
eras.description: A reaction against French fashion, the 'Majo' style celebrated Spanish lower-class flamboyance, characterized by elaborate embroidery and lace. It became a symbol of national identity.
eras.aiImagePrompt: An 18th-century Spanish woman (Maja) wearing a high-waisted skirt, a silk bodice, and a black lace mantilla held up by a high peineta comb, holding a folding fan, painted with Goya-style brushwork.

eras.name: The Rise of Spanish Couture
eras.description: Cristóbal Balenciaga revolutionized silhouettes with architectural precision, moving away from the hourglass figure toward sculptural shapes like the sack dress and the envelope dress.
eras.aiImagePrompt: A 1950s high-fashion editorial featuring a sculptural silk gazar evening gown by Balenciaga, minimalist architectural background, dramatic lighting emphasizing the structural volume of the fabric.

eras.name: La Movida Madrileña
eras.description: A counter-cultural movement following the transition to democracy, characterized by punk influence, gender-bending, and vibrant, kitschy aesthetics led by figures like Pedro Almodóvar.
eras.aiImagePrompt: A group of young Spanish punks on a Madrid street in 1982, wearing colorful mismatched layers, spiked hair, leather vests, and heavy eyeliner, capturing the energy of post-Franco liberation.

eras.name: The Inditex Revolution
eras.description: Spain transformed the global fashion industry through the 'Fast Fashion' model, led by Zara, focusing on rapid supply chains and democratizing luxury-inspired trends.
eras.aiImagePrompt: A minimalist, modern 1990s retail storefront in A Coruña, featuring sleek mannequins in clean, accessible European tailoring and neutral tones.

textiles.name: Almagro Lace
textiles.description: Intricate bobbin lace known for its geometric patterns and durability, historically used for church vestments and mantillas.

textiles.name: Cordovan Leather
textiles.description: A world-renowned leather made from horsehide, known for its non-porous nature and mirror-like finish.

textiles.name: Catalan Cotton
textiles.description: High-quality industrial cotton that fueled the Spanish industrial revolution, used for everything from shirting to heavy canvases.

climate.fashionImplication: Lightweight breathable fabrics like linen and cotton are essential for hot summers, while the 'layering' culture is prominent in the central plateau (Meseta) due to sharp temperature drops at night.

culturalInfluences: The transition from dictatorship to democracy
